feedback.text: In the realm of Digital Fairness, one of the more unfair relationships that currently exist is that of consumers vs game and media providers. Today, game publishers that sell video games which require internet connection to play are shutting down the servers that these video games connect to. In doing this, they render the game unplayable and effectively destroyed. There is very little to no legal recourse for the consumer, which has not been warned of such actions ahead their purchase. There is an ongoing EU Citizens Initiative called Stop Destroying Videogames that currently has 1.4 million signatures, far above the 1 million required for an EU Citizens Initiative. Please consider protecting videogames from destruction under the Digital Fairness Act.
